description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical base constructed from gray stone blocks, giving it a robust and rustic appearance. The well is topped with a wooden gabled roof supported by two vertical wooden beams, providing shelter and protection from debris. A wooden bucket, suspended by a rope, hangs over the open shaft, indicating its function for drawing water. The roof is made of wooden planks, and a simple wooden crank mechanism is visible on one side for lowering and raising the bucket. The interior of the well appears dark, suggesting depth and the presence of water below. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and is typical of wells found in parks, gardens, or village squares.
description: A stone water well with a wooden gabled roof, bucket, and crank mechanism, designed for drawing water. The structure is sturdy and functional, with a rustic, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, and bucket suspended at the center, with the crank mechanism partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the wooden roof, with the bucket hanging inside the well and the crank mechanism clearly visible on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the wooden roof and the bucket, with the supporting beams and stone well structure visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wooden roof and bucket, crank mechanism visible on the left, and the well's stone structure prominent.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled wooden roof made of planks, supported by two beams, no solid cover over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket suspended by a rope, with a visible crank mechanism for raising and lowering.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, supporting the roof and providing a barrier around the well opening.
